repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

comment_data.body: "won't work properly" means whoever reads this in the future will have no idea what that means :)

comment_regular_data.body:
Thanks! Does this allow us to unskip one of these:

```
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/vim_spec.lua @ 2269: lua stdlib vim.opt handles STUPID window things
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 306: vim._with `emsg_silent` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 512: vim._with `hide` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 565: vim._with `horizontal` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 576: vim._with `horizontal` context can be nested
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 591: vim._with `keepalt` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 625: vim._with `keepjumps` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 654: vim._with `keepmarks` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 687: vim._with `keepatterns` context works
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 771: vim._with `noautocmd` context can be nested
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 1040: vim._with `silent` context can be nested
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 1062: vim._with `unsilent` context can be nested
SKIPPED  test/functional/lua/with_spec.lua @ 1496: vim._with can forward command modifiers to user command
```

> Thanks! Does this allow us to unskip one of these:

Applying the change from this PR doesn't seem to make `emsg_silent` flag in `vim._with` work. With `f = function() error('Ooops!') end`, calling `vim._with({ emsg_silent = true }, f)` still throws error while `vim.cmd('silent! lua _G.f()')` does not.
Assuming this is Draft for now
Updated the commit message to better describe what this PR fixes...
can you update `functional/lua/with_spec.lua` , does this need a test

> > Thanks! Does this allow us to unskip one of these:
> 
> Applying the change from this PR doesn't seem to make `emsg_silent` flag in `vim._with` work. With `f = function() error('Ooops!') end`, calling `vim._with({ emsg_silent = true }, f)` still throws error while `vim.cmd('silent! lua _G.f()')` does not.

This hasn't been resolved.

> > > Thanks! Does this allow us to unskip one of these:
> > 
> > 
> > Applying the change from this PR doesn't seem to make `emsg_silent` flag in `vim._with` work. With `f = function() error('Ooops!') end`, calling `vim._with({ emsg_silent = true }, f)` still throws error while `vim.cmd('silent! lua _G.f()')` does not.
> 
> This hasn't been resolved.

This PR isn't intended to fix that problem.

> can you update functional/lua/with_spec.lua , does this need a test

The skipped ```vim._with `emsg_silent` context works``` test also tests this (although falsely until other problem is fixed); so I would personally say no need to add more tests.
Question @justinmk about why [`c78043e`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/34301/changes/c78043e8e6f879970c64d642c6b547f8a154ce88) commit was created onto this PR?
@altermo I think that's from resolving a merge conflict via github web. Feel free to force push

Just FYI, this could have been merged much more quickly if there were a clear problem/solution outline. Because that would reduce the time needed for a reviewer to understand it, and understand if it's the right approach.

I updated the commit msg with my best guess.

title: fix: vim._with silents all cmdline-errors if emsg_silent and not silent
